today.title: 1-1 with Ciara Carroll
today.synopsis: Regular 1-1 rescheduled by Ciara to 10:30 BST so there would be time to discuss any feedback following the Claude Design System announcement made on 29/04. GDCU-8893 is now Done — ideal moment to debrief and agree adoption next steps.
discussion_topics: GDCU-8893 Gambling.com Design System — now Done ✅; discuss community reaction to Claude Design Guidelines announcement and adoption roadmap
GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign — Day 15 Blocked, agree unblocking plan or escalate to Colin Brannigan today
GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery — Chloe flagged 'back and forth with the learning hub' during sprint close; seek resolution to unblock Carlos Blauth's dev work
GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game — Miguel Migneco assigned and In Progress, but off Tuesday 05/05; confirm design progress and any blocker risks before his absence
Sprint 9 priorities — carry-overs: GDCU-8879, GDCU-8846, GDCU-8546, GDCU-8736; WC priorities (FTP game, Cosmo reskin, Group Bets) flagged by Mike to Chloe on 29/04
today.prep_notes: Ciara's DM on 29/04 17:58: 'Hoping the announcement for Claude design came across well and we get feedback!' — the announcement was made. GDCU-8893 moved to Done 30/04 12:24 — a major sprint delivery worth acknowledging. Two Ciara-owned tickets in the In Product queue (GDCU-8736, GDCU-8619) should drive the bulk of the conversation. Check Chloe's 30/04 note: 'back and forth with the learning hub' and 'Tinder [Jess] and Ace audit still have no dev-ready designs.' Mike also mentioned a roadmap discussion in a prior DM (28/04) with Ciara — may surface again today.

action_items.action: SPRINT CLOSE WIN — GDCU-8893 Gambling.com Design System (Highest priority, Ciara Carroll) moved to Done on 30/04 12:24. Ciara's Claude Design Guidelines were announced to the team on 29/04 as confirmed in DM. Publicly endorse the delivery across gdc/core-product channels today and discuss next steps for adoption in the 10:30 1-1. This is the biggest design-tooling milestone of the sprint.

ESCALATE — GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign (Ciara Carroll) is Day 15 Blocked with no movement. Raise directly in the 10:30 1-1. If the blocker cannot be identified and cleared today, escalate to Colin Brannigan. Scope: 3 distinct grid-format mobile-first operator list iterations for UK casino page, with scrollable T&Cs and compare function.

PRODUCTION INCIDENT — RESOLVED ✅. Yesterday at 16:35 BST the UK gambling.com 'highest paying casino' link was 404ing to an Italian author path. Root cause: missing site_id filter in generateById/generateManyById caused the cache (released in Sprint 169) to be populated with Casinos.com and SvenskaCasino data; a duplicate genesis_id from Casinos.com overwrote the correct GDC author path. Harden Wallace released a hotfix at 20:00 BST and ran a UK publish — resolved. Confirm this morning that gambling.com/uk is stable and consider whether a lightweight post-mortem is warranted given the caching change shipped in Sprint 169.

WORLD CUP PRIORITY — GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game created 29/04 and Miguel Migneco is In Progress. This is the flagship FTP initiative: full end-to-end predictor game for WC 2026 with £10k prize pool and IUJ integration (2 chips per match day). CRITICAL: Miguel is off Tuesday 05/05. Check today how far along the design is, what decisions need Mike's input, and ensure no blockers remain before Tuesday. Mike flagged WC as the top priority to Chloe on 29/04 ('prio anything WC related now — FTP game, Cosmo reskin, Group Bets').

Chase Jessica Dordevic on Tinder Style design changes (GDCU-8546). Mike re-requested changes on 30/04 12:39 DM to Chloe: 'frustratingly Jess hasn't made any of the changes on the design file I asked her to make yesterday — I've asked her again.' Requirements: single card (logo, offer, T&Cs); right-swipe or heart → operator landing page post match animation; no AB test; remove 'betting' from 'betting match' label; hide interested/pass buttons once matched. Dev placeholder GDCU-8941 is already created and Blocked waiting on this. Chloe's view: if not dev-ready, it carries to Sprint 9.

Respond to Jessica Dordevic re IUJ Banners (GDCU-8928). Jessica DM 30/04 15:05: 'Let me know about the banners please so I'll check with Ciara after.' Two banners: (1) completing the additional profile page earns chips; (2) recent iPhone winner. Confirm direction so Jessica can proceed and align with Ciara on imagery.

Review and resolve GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery (Ciara Carroll, In Product). Chloe flagged 'back and forth with the learning hub' during the sprint close message (30/04 12:29). Sign off on Ciara's imagery so Carlos Blauth can proceed with dev on GDCU-8733 (Apply Feedback to Learning Hub). Raise in 10:30 1-1 with Ciara and agree a definitive resolution today.

New bug — GDCU-8937 Brand Comparison Tool returning wrong offers for multi-regional geos (e.g. NJ users seeing Michigan offers for Caesars Palace on gambling.com/us). Created 30/04, Backlog, unassigned, Medium priority. Decide whether this enters Sprint 9 and who owns it — potential affiliate revenue impact in US multi-state markets.

Sprint 9 is live — GDCU-8938 (Regression Testing Sprint 9) and GDCU-8939 (Automation Sprint 9) created 30/04. Confirm carry-over prioritisation: GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel (Jess), GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad (Ciara — needs Mike's creative direction), GDCU-8546 Tinder Style (Jess, nearly done), GDCU-8736 Learning Hub (Ciara). IMPORTANT: Ace Assistant shortcode UX ticket is not in any upcoming sprint cycle per Chloe (29/04 DM) — create or prioritise for Sprint 9.

New dev ticket — GDCU-8936 Implement Redesign of Ace Navigation with Global Nav Bar (Backlog, unassigned). Direct follow-on from GDCU-8813 (now Done). Figma and prototype are linked. Assign and plan into Sprint 9. Note: Miguel Migneco (key stakeholder/designer on this) is off Tuesday 05/05.

Check GDCU-8842 Genesis Content Layouts (Harden Wallace, In Progress). Bounced back from In Product at 08:29 BST 30/04 coinciding with the Sprint 169 publish hold. Hotfix was released at 20:00 BST — confirm whether GDCU-8842 was directly related to the caching incident and whether it can now be progressed or closed.

Check GDCU-8834 Cool Tones Update (Harden Wallace, In Progress). Failed QA at 18:31 BST on 29/04 and bounced back to In Progress. Not resolved by yesterday's Jira snapshot. Confirm with Harden whether this is fixed or needs to enter Sprint 9.

GDCU-8879 Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card (Jessica Dordevic, In Progress) — sprint 9 carry-over confirmed by Chloe. Bounced from In Product to In Progress on 29/04. Check with Jessica what changes are being made and when it will return to In Product. Outstanding Chloe feedback: confirm logo name/size sourced from Origins.

Gabriel Cornoiu outstanding intake questions from 23/04 (8 days old): (1) 'Other' option behaviour in offer ordering dropdown; (2) 'new OpList' clarification. Mike responded briefly on 29/04 ('I can do it direct in Greenhouse Gab') but the substantive April 23 questions appear unanswered. Resolve when opportunity arises.

Vic Dadson expected back Tuesday 05/05 after ~2 week sick leave (since ~21/04). Note: Miguel Migneco is also off that same Tuesday. Plan for Vic's re-onboarding and the data engineering options discussion flagged in prior reports. Avoid assuming full-team availability on 05/05.

CARRY FORWARD — GDCU-8846 Cosmo Spins Spring Ad Campaign (Ciara Carroll, To Do). Sprint 9 carry-over confirmed by Chloe. Creative direction from Mike still needed before Ciara can proceed. Josh Kerr feedback: needs rethinking — clearer message, less 'gaming' feel. Mike also flagged 'Cosmo reskin' as a WC priority on 29/04 — confirm if this is the same initiative or a separate reskin.

Directly linked to today's Colin Brannigan walkthrough request. Stakeholders: Colin Brannigan, Chloe Christie, Joshua Smith, Gabriel Cornoiu, Eoin McCarthy. Mike's sign-off required to progress.

Day 15 Blocked (since 2026-04-16). No change in two weeks. Raise in 10:30 1-1 with Ciara today. If blocker cannot be named and cleared, escalate to Colin Brannigan. Scope: 3 distinct grid-format mobile-first operator list iterations for UK casino page with scrollable T&Cs and compare function.

key_context_for_today: 🎉 SPRINT 8/169 CLOSED — 7 tickets completed in 48h including the Highest-priority GDCU-8893 (Design System). Biggest delivery sprint in recent memory.
🚀 SPRINT 9 IS LIVE — GDCU-8938 and GDCU-8939 (Regression + Automation Sprint 9) created 30/04. New sprint underway.

🔥 PRODUCTION INCIDENT RESOLVED — UK gambling.com 404 (Italian author path from caching cross-site pollution) reported 30/04 16:35 BST. Hotfix by Harden Wallace at 20:00 BST. Stable but check this morning.

⚽ GDCU-8935 World Cup Predictor Game — MAJOR new initiative, Miguel In Progress since 29/04. £10k prize pool, full tournament prediction flow, IUJ integration. Miguel off Tuesday 05/05 — check progress today.
💘 GDCU-8546 Tinder Style — Mike frustrated Jess had not made requested changes as of 30/04. Dev placeholder GDCU-8941 Blocked waiting on UX. Chloe: 'Jess will probably close shortly.'
🚫 GDCU-8619 Operator List Redesign — Day 15 Blocked. No change. Escalation required in today's 10:30 Ciara 1-1.
📋 In Product queue down to 2 tickets (GDCU-8736, GDCU-8619) from 6 yesterday — dramatic clearance.
👋 Vic Dadson returns Tuesday 05/05. Miguel Migneco also off that same day.
🐛 Two new unassigned bugs: GDCU-8937 (Brand Comparison Tool multi-geo) and GDCU-8932 (Game Review CTA format). Both need Sprint 9 triage.
📣 Ace Assistant shortcode UX ticket not in any upcoming sprint cycle (Chloe, 29/04 DM) — needs creating or prioritising for Sprint 9.
